title: Wasteland Salvage
description: What the old world left behind — pre-collapse hardware, jury-rigged repairs, scavenged components, and the occasional miracle of something that still works. The condition is part of the story.

entries.result: A solar lantern, working, with a cracked panel that reduces its charge rate but doesn't stop it. Probably the most reliable light source in the region.
A pre-collapse hand radio, battery replaced with a hand-crank generator, range cut to about fifteen kilometers. Someone has scratched a frequency list on the back.
A water purification kit — pre-collapse emergency preparedness issue — with filters rated for six hundred liters. Three have been used. Three remain.
A bolt-action rifle, maintained well, with ammunition counted to forty-seven rounds, which the previous owner counted because they were careful.
A pre-collapse tablet computer that works when connected to power, storing a personal archive — photos, documents, maps — of someone who didn't survive.
A set of surgical tools in a hard case, complete, sterile-wrapped, never opened. The wrapping says they expire eight years from now by the old calendar.
A pre-collapse vehicle battery, fully charged, still viable, worth considerably more than it looks.
A hand-cranked emergency radio that pulls in pre-collapse emergency broadcasts, weather signals, and three stations still broadcasting something.
A pre-collapse first aid kit with most of its contents replaced by post-collapse equivalents by someone who knew what they were substituting.
A set of lockpicks, pre-collapse manufacture, with a post-collapse addition of three custom picks made from piano wire for lock types that didn't exist before.
A solar charging panel, briefcase-sized, folding, able to charge small devices in a few hours of direct light. One hinge is cracked but holds.
A pre-collapse GPS unit, battery replaced, working — but the map data is thirty years old and the roads it references mostly don't exist.
A crossbow, post-collapse manufacture, made by someone who understood both metallurgy and patience. Draws at 150 pounds. Accurate to forty meters.
A pre-collapse multi-tool — high-quality, pre-collapse brand — with every function intact, including two that have become more useful since the collapse.
A radiation dosimeter, working, with a personal log attached that shows the previous owner's cumulative exposure over three years of field work.
A pre-collapse portable generator, gasoline, that runs but loudly, producing enough power for a small building and announcing itself for two hundred meters.
A mechanical watch that keeps perfect time, wound by hand, belonging to someone who understood that knowing the time would matter.
A set of pre-collapse technical manuals — specific machinery — that are the only documentation for equipment still in use in at least two settlements.
A pre-collapse field surgical kit including a bone saw, retractors, and suture material, in a case that has been opened and repacked carefully.
A pair of night vision goggles, battery-dependent, working, rare enough that wearing them in public is an invitation to an event.
A pre-collapse emergency beacon, battery replaced, that will transmit location for seventy-two hours when activated to any receiver on the emergency frequency.
A welding kit — gas, pre-collapse — with enough fuel for about four hours of work. The torch has been modified for field use by someone experienced.
A set of maps, paper, hand-drawn, of the region as it is now rather than as it was — roads that work, water that's clean, dangers that are current.
A pre-collapse compound bow with a full quiver of arrows, half factory, half hand-fletched, maintained by a previous owner who practiced regularly.
A hand-cranked sewing machine that will mend anything and has been doing so for twenty years, clearly loved, clearly used.
A pre-collapse tactical vest, Level IIIA soft armor, intact, offering protection against most handgun rounds. The carrier has been patched in three places.
A set of pre-collapse signal flares — six remaining, sealed, rated for six years past manufacture but probably still viable — with a launcher.
A pre-collapse water testing kit that checks for the fifteen most common post-collapse contaminants. Enough reagent for forty tests.
A compact pre-collapse weather station, solar powered, displaying temperature, humidity, barometric pressure, and a wind reading. Accurate.
A modified pre-collapse civilian pistol — grip replaced, barrel extended, sights upgraded — that shows the work of someone who knew exactly what they wanted from a sidearm.
A pre-collapse emergency mylar blanket set, unused, which most people underestimate until they need one.
A portable distillation kit, post-collapse manufacture, that will turn almost any water source into something drinkable given enough time and fuel.
A pre-collapse trauma kit with tourniquet, hemostatic gauze, chest seals, and decompression needle. Designed for one serious injury. Intact.
A pre-collapse tablet loaded with a complete digital library — fiction, science, technical, historical — with enough storage that it hasn't been fully cataloged.
A set of pre-collapse military field rations — sealed, technically edible — from a type that's been discontinued long enough to be interesting.
A pre-collapse communications jammer, portable, that silences radio in a twenty-meter radius and is completely illegal by every surviving governance framework.
A seed collection — organized, labeled, sealed in foil packets — of pre-collapse crop varieties that haven't been widely grown since.
A pre-collapse fire suppression system canister, unused, that will handle a small structure fire or a very compelling negotiation.
A hand-cranked drill press built from pre-collapse components, portable, that will bore through metal at a rate someone with patience would find acceptable.
A pre-collapse survey instrument — total station, electronic — that still works and would allow accurate land measurement that is currently done by guesswork.
A collection of pre-collapse USB drives with a hand-written inventory of contents, some of which are exactly what the inventory says and some of which are not.
A pre-collapse portable oxygen concentrator, battery-powered, designed for medical use. It runs. It requires significant power.
A set of climbing gear — harness, rope, cams, draws — pre-collapse manufacture, inspected and maintained by someone who knew what failure looked like.
A pre-collapse portable spectrometer that can identify chemical composition of samples. Requires a working laptop to display output. The laptop is not included.
A post-collapse sentry system — motion sensors, pressure plates, linked to a noisemaker — home-built by someone who clearly had experience with what they were trying to avoid.
A pre-collapse personal locator beacon that integrates with pre-collapse satellite infrastructure. The satellites are still up. Whether anyone is still listening is another question.
A hand-pump for fuel transfer, pre-collapse manufacture, that will move diesel or gasoline from any container to any other without electricity.
A pre-collapse shortwave receiver that pulls in broadcasts from several hundred kilometers in favorable conditions, including some sources nobody talks about publicly.
A set of pre-collapse picks and pry bars, construction-grade, that have been rehandled in post-collapse wood and are in better condition than most purpose-made weapons.
A portable air quality monitor, battery-powered, that measures particulates, carbon monoxide, and a list of the most common post-collapse industrial contaminants.
A pre-collapse emergency kit from a vehicle — flares, jumper cables, first aid, reflective triangles — missing the jumper cables, everything else intact.
A hand-built still for alcohol production that is functional, modular, and clearly designed by someone who thought through transportation.
A pre-collapse printed encyclopedia — physical volumes, complete set — in a weatherproof case, weighing more than any similar investment of trade value.
A compact pre-collapse medical ultrasound unit, battery-powered, working, representing a level of diagnostic capability that most settlements don't have.
A pre-collapse suppressed pistol — subsonic ammunition, factory-suppressored — with the provenance of something that wasn't supposed to be in civilian hands before the collapse either.
A set of post-collapse printed currency from a settlement that no longer exists, which a few traders still honor and most don't.
A pre-collapse field chemistry kit for testing soil, water, and biological samples, with reference materials still legible and reagents still viable.
A pre-collapse laptop, working, loaded with software that was cutting-edge thirty years ago and is now either obsolete or irreplaceable depending on what you need it for.
A hand-built methane digester, portable, that converts organic waste into combustible gas at a rate sufficient to cook a meal or run a small generator.
A pre-collapse trauma surgeon's personal kit — not a standard issue kit, assembled by someone specific for their own use — with items that reflect thirty years of experience.
A pre-collapse automotive scanner that will plug into vehicle OBD ports and diagnose electronic systems, representing the ability to maintain pre-collapse vehicles that most mechanics lack.
A set of pre-collapse locksmith's tools including a key duplicator that can operate without power.
A hand-cranked grain mill, post-collapse manufacture, that will process most grains into flour at a rate that makes it worth the effort.
A pre-collapse EMP device, single-use, that will disable electronics in a ten-meter radius. It looks unremarkable. It is the size of a brick.
A set of pre-collapse reference books — medical, technical, agricultural — assembled by someone who anticipated needing them and chose carefully.
A pre-collapse military radio, encrypted, that can communicate with compatible units on military frequencies. The encryption keys are current.
A post-collapse armor suit assembled from pre-collapse vehicle parts, motorcycle gear, and construction equipment. Heavy. Effective. Loud.
A pre-collapse data server, portable, containing records from a specific institution that no longer exists — hospital, government office, university — with thirty years of data.
A compact hand-powered electric generator — bicycle-mount, wheel-friction — that can charge small devices slowly and indefinitely.
A pre-collapse defibrillator — charged, working — that will extend the window of survivability for certain cardiac events and has no other obvious application.
A set of pre-collapse drone components in a case, enough to assemble two functional units, with a controller that works and spare batteries.
A hand-built rifle cartridge reloading press, portable, with dies for the three most common calibers in the region and components for about five hundred rounds.
A pre-collapse environmental remediation kit designed for industrial spill response that is useful for several post-collapse contamination situations it wasn't designed for.
A pre-collapse night vision scope — rifle-mount — that transforms what a competent shooter can do after dark in a way that changes tactical calculations significantly.
A hand-assembled communication relay built from pre-collapse components, designed to extend radio range by capturing and re-broadcasting signals.
A pre-collapse industrial power tool set — battery-operated, fast-charging — representing a level of construction capability that separates settlements that can build from those that can't.
A complete set of pre-collapse topographic maps of the region, paper, laminated, with post-collapse annotations added in three different hands over many years.
A pre-collapse blood typing kit, unused, that can type and cross-match, representing the difference between a blood transfusion working and not working.
A post-collapse electrical system in a portable case — charge controller, battery bank, inverter — that will run small electronics off available solar power indefinitely.
A pre-collapse signal intelligence device that can intercept, record, and analyze radio traffic in a wide frequency range. Its previous owner made notes in the margins of the manual.
A pre-collapse water well drilling kit, hand-powered, designed for field use in developing regions and entirely applicable to post-collapse conditions.
A portable arc welder, pre-collapse, requiring a specific power input that is difficult to supply but that produces professional-grade welds when supplied.
A pre-collapse automated external defibrillator with audio instructions and enough battery for approximately fifteen shocks. It talks. This startles people.
A set of post-collapse improvised explosive devices — small, effective, safe to handle if you know what you're doing — from a cache clearly assembled by someone who did.
A pre-collapse seed sprouting kit designed for growing food in confined spaces — space program technology, effectively — that works year-round regardless of climate.
A pre-collapse antibiotic supply — sealed, cold-storage maintained, still within extended viable window — for conditions that would otherwise be fatal in post-collapse medical settings.
A hand-assembled alarm system — pressure plates, tripwires, noisemakers — in a bag, ready to deploy, representing about six hours of field setup.
A pre-collapse portable X-ray unit, battery-operated, that will produce diagnostic-quality images and represents the kind of medical capability that changes what's survivable.
A pre-collapse solar panel charge controller — industrial grade — that will integrate mismatched panels into a functional charging system with efficiency that improvised systems can't match.
A set of pre-collapse precision machining tools — small, portable — that allow manufacturing of small mechanical components to specifications that most post-collapse smiths can't match.

A pre-collapse insulin pump, loaded, intended for a specific patient profile that may or may not match anyone currently alive, representing a category of medical need most post-collapse medicine ignores.
A handheld chemical agent detector, pre-collapse military issue, that identifies common toxic industrial chemicals and several more specific compounds it was designed to find.
A pre-collapse satellite phone, charged, that can reach three numbers stored in memory — two are disconnected, one answers.
A complete pre-collapse ham radio station in portable cases — transceiver, power supply, antenna components — capable of global range on HF bands that are still active.
A pre-collapse self-contained underwater breathing apparatus, tanks full, maintained, representing mobility in flooded areas that most salvagers don't have.
A working pre-collapse 3D printer — filament-based, small build volume — that can produce mechanical components in plastic with accuracy that changes what's repairable.
A pre-collapse gene sequencer, portable, that can analyze biological samples for specific markers. Its previous owner left an analysis log showing what she was looking for.
A pre-collapse military tactical computer, field-grade, with encrypted communications, mapping, and targeting integration that still connects to active military satellites.
A working pre-collapse power armor exoskeleton, partially charged, with a damage profile indicating it has been through significant use and survived.

A pre-collapse autonomous maintenance drone, still on its original programming, found in a sealed facility. It has been maintaining the facility for thirty years. The facility is intact. Whatever the drone was protecting is still there.
